memoryArtifacts.headline: Sir Andrew Robert Herries 1rst Lord Herries  1477-1513
memoryArtifacts.description:
Robert, second Lord Herries of Terregles. He married Lady Janet Douglas, daughter of Archibald, fifth Earl of Angus, Killed at Glodden, 1513.
RE. Book of Carlaverock Vol 1 PP 586.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Andrew Robert Herries 2nd Lord Herries於1477年出生在蘇格蘭吉德布萊次爾特雷格萊斯時，他的父親Herbert Herries 6th Lord Herries of Terregles17歲，母親Mariëtte Carlyle Baroness of  Herr15歲。他於1494年與Beatrice結婚。他去世於1513年9月9日在英國諾圖波藍德布藍斯頓享年36歲。

nameMeanings.name: Herries
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  Norman, Scottish, English: locative name from Hérils in Trévières (Calvados). The name in England may have died out before the end of the medieval period, but survived into the modern period in Scotland.
2 English: variant of Herry with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. Compare Harris .

variants.name: Herry
meanings: Scots: Herry, to plunder, most properly to plunder birds' nests

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

nameMeanings.name: Andrew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the Greek name Andreas, a short form of any of various compound names derived from andr- ‘man, warrior’. In the New Testament this is the name of the first disciple to be called by Jesus. After the Resurrection, St Andrew preached in Asia Minor and Greece. He is traditionally believed to have been crucified at Patras in Achaia. He was one of the most popular saints of the Middle Ages and was adopted as the patron of Scotland, Russia, and Greece. It has long been among the most popular boys' names in the English-speaking world, especially in Scotland. Its popularity in England was further enhanced by its use as a British royal name for Prince Andrew ( b. 1960 ), the Duke of York.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Andrew Robert Herries 2nd Lord Herries was born in 1477, in Terregles, Kirkcudbrightshire, Scotland, his father, Herbert Herries 6th Lord Herries of Terregles, was 17 and his mother, Mariëtte Carlyle Baroness of  Herr, was 15. He married Beatrice in 1494. He died on 9 September 1513, in Branxton, Northumberland, England, at the age of 36.

ko.lifeSummary: Andrew Robert Herries 2nd Lord Herries(이)가 1477년 스컷랜드 커kc드브라이츠히르 터레글레스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herbert Herries 6th Lord Herries of Terregles(은)는 17세였고, 그의 어머니 Mariëtte Carlyle Baroness of  Herr(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1494년 Beatrice(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1513년 9월 9일 영국 노르스벌랜드 브랜스턴에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Beatrice в 1494 г. Он умер 9 сентября 1513 г. в местности бранкстон, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 36 лет.
